displayTitle: Drive Bends the Rules on Steel
kicker: ET LEVEL II // AC FIELD STRENGTH

beats.narration: Drive sets the AC field strength your coil pushes into the part. On nonmagnetic metal it scales cleanly. On steel it can break the test.

payload.title: DRIVE BENDS THE RULES ON STEEL
payload.sub: One knob, two completely different physics.
payload.panelLabel: AC FIELD STRENGTH AT THE COIL

beats.narration: On aluminum or titanium the system is linear. Double the drive and every signal rises six decibels — but the impedance point, phase, and depth stay parked.
On steel, permeability tracks field strength. A strong drive sweeps a fat minor hysteresis loop, so a clean sine returns peaked — built of odd harmonics.

payload.heading: Steel adds the B–H curve to the circuit
payload.note: LOW DRIVE: LENS LOOP · HIGH DRIVE: FAT LOOP, 3RD HARMONIC

beats.narration: Too much field on steel shows five ways. Learn them as diagnostics — bump the drive one step, and watch what moves.

payload.heading: Symptoms of excessive drive on steel

beats.narration: So classify the metal first. Run nonmagnetic high, run unsaturated steel low, record the setting, and re-standardize after any change.

payload.sub: Drive is an essential variable — change it, re-prove it.
